summary.intent: Fix subSessionId never being populated in the DB when spawning Claude subsessions via AppleScript, and additionally link the spawned session to its parent session.
summary.outcome: Both issues resolved: open item IDs are passed via GOSSAMER_SPAWN_OPEN_ITEMS env var and parent session via GOSSAMER_SPAWN_PARENT_SESSION, with the SessionStart hook handler reading these to populate subSessionId and agentParents in the DB.

repo: AppleScript terminal spawning (osascript) is fire-and-forget — no return value is available, so session IDs cannot be captured synchronously at spawn time.
Claude Code passes the launching process's env vars to hook subprocesses, enabling env-var-based communication between the spawn caller and the spawned session's hooks.
SessionStart hook in handler.ts is the correct place to perform post-spawn DB linkage, as it fires as soon as a new Claude session starts.
agentParents in state is the mechanism for tracking parent-child session relationships.
The auto-link heuristic (30s window) is the fallback for parent detection; explicit GOSSAMER_SPAWN_PARENT_SESSION takes priority over it.

code.finding: Spawn endpoint builds the terminal command string; open item IDs and parent session ID are injected as env var prefixes: `GOSSAMER_SPAWN_OPEN_ITEMS='1,2,3' GOSSAMER_SPAWN_PARENT_SESSION='<id>' claude 'prompt'`
SessionStart handler reads GOSSAMER_SPAWN_OPEN_ITEMS and GOSSAMER_SPAWN_PARENT_SESSION from process.env, updates open item subSessionId, and sets agentParents
spawnSession API function extended with optional parentSessionId parameter
Spawn call now passes session.sessionId as parentSessionId

workflow: Running a build after edits is a fast way to catch type errors in TypeScript projects before testing.
When async/synchronous return is impossible (fire-and-forget shell spawns), env vars passed to the child process are an effective out-of-band communication channel.

friction: The root cause (AppleScript fire-and-forget returning no usable value) required a non-obvious workaround rather than a direct fix.
Multiple files required coordinated changes (api.ts, SessionDetail.tsx, server.ts, handler.ts) for each feature, increasing risk of missed updates.
open_items: No validation or cleanup if GOSSAMER_SPAWN_OPEN_ITEMS contains invalid/stale IDs that no longer exist in the DB.
The 30s auto-link heuristic for parent detection remains in place as a fallback — its interaction with the new explicit mechanism should be monitored for edge cases.
No test coverage added for the new env-var-based linking logic in the SessionStart handler.
